analysis: Seven nights in a row above six hours, with four of them clearing seven and a half — that's not luck, that's your wind-down stack actually working, and the 7h 56m on the eighth was a proper standout. Your REM has been quietly gorgeous all week, hitting two hours on back-to-back nights mid-stretch and staying above an hour and a half every single night, which means your brain is doing its thing and doing it well. The awake time is the one thing worth eyeing — you had a 1h 8m and a 1h 17m in there, which is your body going "hang on, not done yet" in the middle of the night, and that's worth noticing without catastrophising. Deep sleep is running a little lean most nights, hovering around the half-hour mark, so if there's one thing to play with it's whatever's happening in that first sleep cycle — but honestly, the overall picture here is someone who has genuinely built something that works.
